title: Abort from re-initializing and repopulating an `AtomicDict`
author: devdanzin
body:
Running the code below will cause an abort:

```python
import cereggii
a = cereggii.AtomicDict()

# Prime the AtomicDict to len=100
for x in range(100):
    a[x] = x

# Re-init to len=1
a.__init__({1:2})
print(f"{len(a)=}")
print(f"{list(a.fast_iter())=}")

# Delete the lone key and get len=100
del a[1]
print(f"Should be empty, but {len(a)=}")

# Try to repopulate and get an abort
for x in range(100):
    a[x] = x
```

Message:
```
Fatal Python error: _Py_CheckSlotResult: Slot __setitem__ of type cereggii.AtomicDict failed without setting an exception
Python runtime state: initialized

Stack (most recent call first):
  File "/home/danzin/scratch.py", line 18 in <module>

Program received signal SIGABRT, Aborted.
```
